summary: Prophylactic vaccines can prevent various types of bacterial illness but not all; they do target some significant bacterial pathogens effectively such as those causing pneumonia from Streptococcus pneumoniae strains. The primary focus should be on identifying which bacterium-specific vaccinations have been proven effective through rigorous scientific study before recommending them broadly across populations
